title: Decide equal-priority task ranking policy
description: Resolve the lower-priority question of how equal-priority actionable tasks should be ordered. This task records a product decision only; it does not expand the operational correctness release with a new schema and UI field. If explicit rank is justified, create a separately phased implementation task with migration and surface requirements.
acceptance: An ADR compares priority-plus-id, priority-plus-created_at-plus-id, and optional explicit rank using deterministic behavior, migration cost, and user-control evidence.
The selected current ordering is documented consistently for in-memory and index-backed queries.
If explicit rank is accepted, a new implementation task is created with schema, mutation, CLI, MCP, dashboard, migration, and deterministic-test acceptance criteria.
The decision records why no deferred status is added unless todo-as-backlog proves insufficient after the readiness correction.
This decision runs after and does not block the correction release; the umbrella milestone waits for the decision record.
notes: Separated from generated-artifact policy because the two decisions govern different systems and critical paths. It follows the correction release so optional product strategy cannot delay correctness.
